seo.title: Building API design and documentation with FastAPI and Ex...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for API design and documentation with FastAPI and Express.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to building API-first products with focus on implementation, tooling, and production considerations. Follow these steps to create a scalable, well-documented API product that addresses common developer pain points.
prerequisites: Unkey or equivalent API key management service

steps.title: Define API endpoints with OpenAPI specification
steps.explanation: Create a formal OpenAPI 3.1 document that defines all endpoints, request/response formats, and security requirements. Use this as the single source of truth for implementation.

code.content:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Product API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /products:
    get:
      summary: List products
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successful response
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: object
                  properties:
                    id: { type: string }
                    name: { type: string }

pitfalls: Not updating the spec after implementation changes
Using inconsistent data types between spec and code

steps.title: Implement authentication and rate limiting
steps.explanation: Integrate Unkey for API key management and configure rate limits using middleware. Apply these to all endpoints with proper error responses for unauthorized/overaged requests.
pitfalls: Not handling rate limit exhaustion gracefully
Using weak authentication mechanisms without rotation

steps.title: Implement versioning strategy
steps.explanation: Use URL versioning (e.g., /v1/products) and maintain backward compatibility. Document deprecation schedules for outdated versions.
pitfalls: Changing request/response formats without deprecation warnings
Not maintaining multiple versions in parallel

steps.title: Generate and maintain documentation
steps.explanation: Use Swagger UI or ReDoc to auto-generate documentation from the OpenAPI spec. Set up CI/CD to validate spec consistency with implementation.
pitfalls: Not testing documentation against actual endpoints

steps.title: Implement usage-based pricing
steps.explanation: Integrate Stripe Billing to track API usage metrics. Create pricing tiers that align with your monetization strategy.
pitfalls: Ignoring proration rules for tiered pricing

content.conclusion: By following this implementation sequence, you'll create a production-ready API product that balances developer experience with business requirements. Ensure all components are tested in staging environments before public deployment.
